instructions.original_snippets: Identify the work type (empirical paper, literature review, proposal, etc.) and scope before selecting evaluation dimensions.
Load references/evaluation_framework.md for detailed criteria and rubrics before scoring any dimension.
NEVER assign a score without supporting it with specific evidence from the work being evaluated.
ALWAYS evaluate all applicable dimensions systematically — do not skip dimensions because they seem weak.
Frame weaknesses as opportunities for improvement, not failures; use the 5-point scale consistently across all dimensions.
NEVER conflate quantitative scores with the narrative assessment — scores are optional supplementary data, not the primary output.
Adjust evaluation expectations based on work stage: a draft proposal is held to lower standards than a submission-ready manuscript.
